title: JAL plane makes emergency landing at Kansai due to oil leak
summary: A Japan Airlines flight from Haneda was diverted to Kansai airport after an oil leak; no injuries were reported and the runway was temporarily closed.

title: SoftBank Hawks player Akira Nakamura announces his retirement
summary: The veteran outfielder confirmed he will retire at the end of the season, expressing pride in his career and gratitude towards his teammates.

title: Tokyo stock market recovers after initial drop, closes up 1010 points
summary: The Nikkei 225 closed at 69,744 points, recovering from a drop of more than 1,100 points in the morning, as expectations of a US rate hike receded.

title: Japan's long-term interest rate rises to 2.81%, highest in 30 years
summary: The 10-year bond yield reached its highest level since 1996, sparking warnings about the expansionary fiscal policy of the Takaichi government.

title: World Cup results: Spain advances, Croatia says goodbye, and Japan falls to Brazil
summary: On the day, Spain defeated Austria with two goals from Oyarzabal, Croatia was eliminated despite Modric's efforts, and Japan's defeat raises doubts about the coach.

title: Multiple sexual crime cases in Japan: musician, pilot, and teacher investigated
summary: Justice accused former ONE OK ROCK member Bobbie of rape, sought prison for an ANA captain for groping, and arrested a Hokkaido teacher for taking illicit photos.

title: Japan boosts railway tourism with historical and scenic initiatives
summary: Initiatives promote the preservation of the Shinkansen Series 0, a luxury tourist train to Mount Koya, and the conversion of the Oigawa line into an attraction with a rack system.

title: Germany prosecutes former Ukrainian soldier for Nord Stream sabotage
summary: The German prosecutor's office filed charges against a former Ukrainian officer for the explosion of the gas pipelines in the Baltic Sea, straining bilateral relations.

title: LDP criticizes opposition for boycotting parliamentary deliberations
summary: The ruling party called the opposition's refusal to debate 'outdated,' while leader Tamiki pointed out that the government evades discussion.

title: Japan plans to increase residence permit fees from October
summary: The government plans to significantly raise immigration fees, with limited exemptions for asylum seekers, drawing criticism.

title: Japan's Trade Commission closes investigation into Broadcom
summary: The regulator determined that Broadcom did not violate antitrust laws in the sale of virtualization software, closing the case without sanctions.

title: Princess Kako visits a school for the blind in Yokohama and plays the tambourine with students
summary: Her Imperial Highness interacted with visually impaired students of the Yokohama Kunmo Institute, participating in a joint concert.

title: Roki Sasaki gives up 6 runs in 3 innings, but Dodgers rally
summary: The Japanese pitcher allowed three home runs in three innings, but the Dodgers' offense, with a hit from Ohtani, staged a 12-run comeback.

title: 5th anniversary of Atami landslide: 25 displaced and a ceremony
summary: On the fifth anniversary of the tragedy that killed 27, a tribute was held at the site, while 25 families remain in temporary housing.

title: Bear sightings in Japan lead to reinforced safety measures
summary: After a bear appeared at Kyoto University, the Environment Minister urged the implementation of more preventive measures in national parks.

title: Iran warns against intervention in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Tehran rejected the meeting of eleven Arab countries with the U.S. and threatened to act if its waters are interfered with, raising regional tensions.

title: Karamucho snacks and Yu-Pack service prices to rise from October
summary: Koikeya will raise the price of 20 products and Japan Post will increase parcel rates by 10% due to rising transport and raw material costs.

title: Japan tightens immigration rules and requires integration program for residency
summary: Authorities intensify inspections at restaurants hiring undocumented foreigners and plan to require Japanese language studies for permanent residency.

title: Two Japanese local governments suffer data leaks and cyberattacks
summary: Kitakyushu confirmed the data leak of 1,139 people, and Wakayama reported a mass sending of fraudulent emails from its server after illegal access.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi returns from India, leadership under analysis
summary: After his tour with bilateral meetings, analyses emerge about his non-hereditary leadership style and his ability to unify the government.
